title: List all tasks

story.i_want_to: see all my tasks displayed in a formatted table
story.so_that: I can quickly review what I need to do and what I've already done

rationale: Listing tasks is how users interact with the app most frequently. A clear, well-formatted display makes the tool pleasant to use and helps users scan their tasks at a glance.

acceptance_criteria.description: Running `todo list` displays all tasks in a formatted table with columns: ID, Status, Description, Created.
Status is displayed as a visual indicator: '[ ]' for pending, '[x]' for done.
Created date is displayed in a human-readable short format (e.g., '2026-03-25').
When there are no tasks, the app prints 'No tasks yet. Add one with: todo add "your task"'.
Tasks are displayed in order of ID (ascending).
The table displays a header row ('ID  Status  Description  Created') with a separator line below it.
Descriptions longer than the available terminal width are truncated with '...' to maintain table alignment. Uses shutil.get_terminal_size() to determine available width.

test_cases.name: List tasks shows formatted output
preconditions: tmp_dir with tasks.json containing 2 tasks (one pending, one done)
expected_results: Tasks are sorted by ID ascending

test_cases.name: List empty tasks shows helpful message
List displays correct status indicators
preconditions: tmp_dir with tasks.json containing one pending and one done task

test_cases.name: Table includes header row and separator
preconditions: tmp_dir with tasks.json containing at least 1 task
expected_results: First line of output contains column headers (ID, Status, Description, Created)
Second line contains a separator (e.g., dashes)

test_cases.name: Long descriptions are truncated
preconditions: tmp_dir with tasks.json containing a task with a 200-character description
steps: Run `python todo.py list` with HOME=tmp_dir in a terminal with 80-column width

technical_notes: Format output using string formatting (f-strings) with fixed-width columns
Always display header row + separator line
Use shutil.get_terminal_size() to detect terminal width for truncation
Truncate descriptions: desc[:max_width-3] + '...' when exceeding available space
Date formatting: task['created_at'][:10] gives YYYY-MM-DD from ISO format
For Unicode/emoji width issues: document as known limitation for v1, can use wcwidth library later
